slides.title: Product strategy priorities
slides.subtitle: Where focus creates the clearest strategic advantage

slides.title: The roadmap should concentrate on fewer, more compounding bets
slides.subtitle: Focus is a strategic quality decision
bullets: Reduce initiative spread across too many low-conviction themes.
Increase sequencing discipline between platform, growth, and customer-facing work.
Create clearer executive decision points around investments and trade-offs.

cards.title: Platform reliability
cards.body: Protect trust and operational efficiency by investing in stability before expanding surface area.

cards.title: Workflow adoption
cards.body: Prioritize features that increase repeat usage in the core daily workflow.

cards.title: Decision visibility
cards.body: Improve instrumentation so leadership can see product performance earlier and more clearly.

slides.title: 90-day execution sequence
slides.subtitle: A tighter roadmap needs deliberate sequencing

timeline_items.title: Stabilize the foundation
timeline_items.body: Resolve the reliability gaps that keep slowing down roadmap confidence.

timeline_items.title: Concentrate adoption work
timeline_items.body: Prioritize the workflow surfaces that create repeat usage in the core product loop.

timeline_items.title: Instrument decisions
timeline_items.body: Improve visibility so leadership can judge product bets faster and with less ambiguity.

slides.title: Recommendation
slides.quote: A strong roadmap is not a list of ideas — it is a sequence of deliberate choices that compounds over time.
